demographics: I am 57 years old. I am female. I am White. I am divorced. I completed high school (12th grade). My family income is $40,000 to $49,999. I am a not very strong Republican. I am slightly conservative. I never attend religious services.

events.event_text: I was laid off from my job at the manufacturing plant where I'd worked for 15 years. It was a shock, and I wasn't sure how I was going to provide for my family.
When I was 35, I was diagnosed with breast cancer. It was a terrifying time, especially being a single mother with no immediate family nearby for support.

At 27, I married my high school sweetheart. We were both excited to finally start our life together. However, within a year, it became clear that our differences were too great, and after much heartache, we decided to get a divorce.

At 38, I volunteered at a local community center to keep busy after my divorce. There, I met people from all walks of life, which opened my eyes to different perspectives and deepened my sense of empathy.
I had been struggling to find a decent job after my divorce, but things turned around when I met a generous employer who paid a fair wage. I started saving and even managed to buy my first house.

After years of trying, I was finally able to adopt a child from a distant country. Raising her has been challenging, but it has also deepened my understanding of the world and given me a new appreciation for cultural diversity.

compressed_summaries.summary: This person was laid off from their long-term manufacturing job, causing financial uncertainty. At 35, they were diagnosed with breast cancer while being a single mother without nearby family support. They married their high school sweetheart at 27, but the marriage ended in divorce a year later due to irreconcilable differences.

This person married their high school sweetheart at 27, but divorced a year later due to irreconcilable differences. At 38, they volunteered at a community center, broadening their perspectives and empathy, and later found stable employment and bought a house. They adopted a child from another country, experiencing challenges and cultural growth.

persona_card: "Divorced and resilient, this 57-year-old White woman, laid off from a long-term manufacturing job, is a not very strong Republican with slightly conservative leanings. Despite never attending religious services, she holds moderately traditional moral views. She supports economic redistribution and acknowledges structural racial issues, yet maintains a guarded trust in institutions and feels open towards social outgroups."
